subject: [PATCH v1 4/9] fuse: use enum types for header copying

2026-03-24.patch_summary: This patch improves the interface for copying headers in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation by introducing enum types to identify which part of the header needs to be copied. This simplifies both kernel-space and user-space header addresses copying, particularly when kernel-managed buffer rings are added. The patch adds a new enum type `fuse_uring_header_type` with values for different header types, and modifies two inline functions `copy_header_to_ring` and `copy_header_from_ring` to use this enum type. This change will make the code more maintainable and easier to understand.
